figures.paper_intent: Demonstrate accumulated reward and reward-per-task evolution during training under single hyperparameter configuration
mechanism_being_tested: Does the agent converge to stable policy?
At what episode does convergence stabilize?

sub_figures.name: Accumulated Reward Over Training
sub_figures.required_data: Accumulated reward per episode (episodes 1-5000) at lr=7e-7, gamma=0.99
sub_figures.reason: Single-config real data mechanistically valid. Hyperparameter robustness requires sweep (not run).

sub_figures.name: Reward Per Task Over Training
sub_figures.required_data: Reward-per-task per episode at lr=7e-7, gamma=0.99
sub_figures.reason: Valid single-config real data. Sweep panels require additional runs.

sub_figures.name: Learning-Rate and Discount-Factor Sweep Panels
sub_figures.available_data: No - only single config (lr=7e-7, gamma=0.99) executed
sub_figures.reason: Requires 5-10 additional full 5000-episode runs. Not executed.

figures.caption_honesty: Must state 'single-configuration results; sweep panels require additional hyperparameter tuning (future work)'

figures.paper_intent: Show action distribution and sensitivity to system parameters (arrival, agent count, capacity, rate)
mechanism_being_tested: Which actions (L/H/V) does learned policy prefer?
How robust across system parameter variations?

sub_figures.name: Action Distribution
sub_figures.required_data: Real action counts from trained agent evaluation
sub_figures.reason: Real distribution from fixed environment. Sweep generalization is optional.

sub_figures.name: Reward by Task Arrival Probability
sub_figures.available_data: No - only single arrival profile evaluated
sub_figures.reason: Requires re-evaluation under different arrival profiles. Not executed.

sub_figures.name: Reward by DRL Agent Count
sub_figures.reason: Requires separate evaluation configs. Not executed.

sub_figures.name: Reward by CPU Capacity
sub_figures.available_data: No - only single capacity config evaluated

sub_figures.name: Reward by Offloading Data Rate
sub_figures.available_data: No - only single rate config evaluated

figures.caption_honesty: Must state 'Action distribution from fixed environment (9a real); system parameter sensitivity requires additional evaluation campaigns (9b-9e future work)'

figures.paper_intent: Demonstrate learned agent achieves lower delay/drop than baselines; validate DRL approach
mechanism_being_tested: Is learned routing effective for deadline-aware scheduling?

sub_figures.name: Average Delay vs Baselines
sub_figures.required_data: Average latency (slots) for 7 policies: candidate, fixed_local, fixed_horizontal, fixed_vertical, random_legal, capacity_split_oracle
sub_figures.reason: All baseline and candidate metrics fully available and reconciled (delta=0.0).

sub_figures.name: Drop Ratio vs Baselines
sub_figures.required_data: Drop ratio (deadline violations) for same 7 policies
sub_figures.reason: All metrics fully available from reconciled simulation runs.

figures.caption_honesty: Can state 'Comparison of delay and drop ratio against implemented baselines, generated fully from simulation outputs.'

figures.paper_intent: Demonstrate LSTM layer is necessary; contrast with and without LSTM
mechanism_being_tested: Does LSTM provide significant delay reduction?
How much performance is due to recurrence?
Is LSTM contribution measurable and substantial?

sub_figures.name: Delay Trend (With LSTM)
sub_figures.reason: Real performance with LSTM. Without-LSTM comparison requires ablation.

sub_figures.name: Delay Trend (Without LSTM)
sub_figures.required_data: Delay measurements from feedforward (no-LSTM) training run for 5000 episodes
sub_figures.reason: Ablation requires separate 5000-episode training run. Not executed.

figures.caption_honesty: Must state 'LSTM-enabled model delay (11a real); no-LSTM ablation not yet executed and is marked as future work (11b).'
